message:
feat(mvcc): add HTM Guard state machine skeleton for flat combiner fast-path (bd-77l3t Phase 1)

Add the full HTM (Hardware Transactional Memory) guard infrastructure
to the flat combiner, implementing the state machine, EWMA abort-rate
monitor, and PRAGMA-based configuration interface. Phase 1 is a
skeleton: no actual HTM intrinsics are wired, so the guard always
resolves to HTM_UNAVAILABLE and all threads continue using the existing
lock path. Zero behavior change in this phase.

State machine (6 states, stored in AtomicU8):
- NOT_PROBED → initial state, triggers lazy CPU probe on first use
- AVAILABLE → HTM fast-path is active (future phases only)
- UNAVAILABLE → CPU lacks HTM support (Phase 1 always lands here)
- BLACKLISTED → CPU has known-buggy HTM (e.g., Haswell i3 stepping)
- DISABLED → dynamically disabled due to high abort rate, with
  exponential-backoff cooldown (5s initial, 60s max) before re-probe
- USER_DISABLED → explicitly disabled via PRAGMA fsqlite_htm_mode=off

EWMA abort-rate monitor:
- Tracks attempts and aborts in sliding windows of 1000 operations
- Computes exponentially weighted moving average (alpha=0.3) in
  fixed-point [0..10000] representation
- Transitions AVAILABLE → DISABLED when rate exceeds 50% threshold
- Transitions DISABLED → AVAILABLE after cooldown expiry

HtmAbortClassification:
- Categorizes abort status codes into Conflict, Capacity, Explicit,
  Retry, Nested, Debug, and Unknown reasons
- Reports retry eligibility based on abort category

PRAGMA interface:
- fsqlite_htm_mode: off/on/auto (USER_DISABLED ↔ AVAILABLE/auto-probe)
- fsqlite_htm_status: read-only JSON with state, EWMA rate, abort
  counters, cooldown info, and feature flag
- HtmPragmaValues serializable struct for JSON output

Global counters (AtomicU64):
- htm_attempts, htm_commits, htm_aborts_conflict, htm_aborts_capacity,
  htm_aborts_explicit, htm_aborts_retry, htm_aborts_nested,
  htm_aborts_debug, htm_aborts_other
- Wired to tracing events at debug level for observability

Tests:
- State machine transition coverage (NOT_PROBED → UNAVAILABLE)
- EWMA calculation verification
- Abort classification for all reason categories
- PRAGMA round-trip (off → on → auto → status JSON)
- Global counter increment and snapshot

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
